Disadvantage Communities


Show code

ejscreen_data <- dishawaii %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    env_burden = any(c_across(c(P_PM25,P_OZONE,P_DSLPM,P_RSEI_AIR,P_PTRAF,
P_LDPNT,P_PNPL,P_PRMP,P_PTSDF,P_UST,P_PWDIS,P_NO2,P_DWATER,P_D2_PM25,P_D2_OZONE,P_D2_DSLPM,P_D2_RSEI_AIR,P_D2_PTRAF,P_D2_LDPNT,P_D2_PNPL,P_D2_PRMP,P_D2_PTSDF,P_D2_UST,P_D2_PWDIS,P_D2_NO2,P_D2_DWATER)) >= 90, na.rm = TRUE),
    soc_vulnerable = any(c_across(c(P_PEOPCOLORPCT,P_LOWINCPCT,P_UNEMPPCT,P_DISABILITYPCT,P_LINGISOPCT,P_LESSHSPCT,P_UNDER5PCT,P_OVER64PCT,P_LIFEEXPPCT)) >= 90, na.rm = TRUE),
    DAC_flag = as.integer(env_burden & soc_vulnerable)
  ) %>%

title: Unequal Recreational Losses? Revealed Preference Evidence of Campsite Closures in Hawaiʻi
text:
Zip to Tract
Better way to match the data is by using the HUD USPS ZIP Code Crosswalk Files. This will link the zip codes to census track. This especially useful to integrate datasets that report by ZIP (e.g. user addresses) with data reported by census tract (like CEJST).


Show code
t=merge(zip_tract_032022,average_welfare_loss_per_zip, by.x = "ZIP", by.y = "zip")

block_data <- blockIRA %>%
    mutate(tract_geoid = substr(ID, 1, 11)) %>%
    group_by(tract_geoid) %>%
    summarise(
        num_yes = sum(Disadvanta == "Yes", na.rm = TRUE),
        num_no  = sum(Disadvanta == "No", na.rm = TRUE),
        mixed_response = if_else(num_yes > 0 & num_no > 0, TRUE, FALSE),      CEJST = sum(CEJST == "Yes", na.rm = TRUE)
    )

block_data$CEJST <- ifelse(block_data$CEJST > 0, 1, 0)


t=merge(t,block_data, by.x = "TRACT", by.y = "tract_geoid")

t=merge(t,ejscreen_data, by.x = "TRACT", by.y = "ID")

title: Unequal Recreational Losses? Revealed Preference Evidence of Campsite Closures in Hawaiʻi
text:
Data Visual

CEJST
Here we have the spread of observed Justice40 coverage, which reflects the proportion of ZIP codes or census tracts in our dataset that are identified as disadvantaged communities under the CEJST tool.
These communities are eligible to receive targeted federal investments under the Justice40 Initiative.


Show code
ggplot(t, aes(x = island, y = avg_welfare_loss_zip, fill = factor(CEJST))) +
    geom_boxplot(position = "dodge", aes(group = interaction(island, CEJST))) +
    scale_fill_manual(
        name = "CEJST Disadvantaged",
        values = c("0" = "gray70", "1" = "darkred"),
        labels = c("0" = "Not Disadvantaged", "1" = "Disadvantaged")
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(
        x = "Island",
        y = "Average Welfare Loss per ZIP",
        title = "Welfare Loss by Island and CEJST Designation"
    )











EJSceen
EJScreen, developed by the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), is a screening tool that provides percentile rankings for environmental and demographic indicators, such as air pollution, proximity to hazardous sites, poverty, and limited English proficiency, across census tracts in the U.S. It is primarily used for identifying areas with potential environmental justice concerns, but it does not designate any community as “disadvantaged.”
Instead, it enables comparisons across regions and supports risk assessments, permitting decisions, and community advocacy. In contrast, the Climate and Economic Justice Screening Tool (CEJST), developed by the White House Council on Environmental Quality (CEQ), is designed specifically to identify disadvantaged communities (DACs) eligible for benefits under the Justice40 Initiative, which aims to direct 40% of federal climate and infrastructure investments to these communities. CEJST is a binary approach labeling each census tract as either disadvantaged or not. While EJScreen is more detailed and flexible for analysis, CEJST provides a clear policy designation to guide funding and investment.
We use the State Percentiles here.
The U.S. percentiles are computed based on the block groups nationwide. The state percentiles are calculated within each individual state. EJScreen focuses on the U.S. percentiles as a way to visualize all results in common units. The U.S. percentile uses the U.S. block groups as the basis of comparison. The state percentile was also calculated based on the block groups of a given state (or the District of Columbia or Puerto Rico). The national or state mean value was calculated as the average of the block groups with data for that indicator, within the respective geographic scope.

Social Vulnerabilities
Using EJScreen gives us flexibility where we can both examine the social factors and environmental factors. Here we will look at the social vulnerabilities of 90th percentiles within the state who are identified using :

This script will run a RUM model on our data assuming a stay at home option and compare it to our result of the MDCEV


This function calculate the rum welfare manual and is used for each year to get a comparison to the MDCEV.

calc_rum_welfare_manual <- function(target_park) {
  
  beta_price <- coef(rum_mle)["price"]
  
  # 1. Baseline Logsum
  ls_base <- df_math %>%
    group_by(trip_id) %>%
    summarise(ls_base = log(sum(exp(V))), .groups = "drop")
  
  # 2. Policy Logsum (Removing the park one by one and then use the consumer surplus using closed form based on the marginal utility of income
  ls_policy <- df_math %>%
    filter(park_name != target_park) %>%
    group_by(trip_id) %>%
    summarise(ls_policy = log(sum(exp(V))), .groups = "drop")
  
  # 3. Calculate CV for EVERY individual trip
  comparison <- left_join(ls_base, ls_policy, by = "trip_id") %>%
    mutate(cv = (ls_policy - ls_base) / -beta_price) # this is trying to compare to the MDCEV where we close and re-estimate
  
  # 4. Calculate Statistics across all trips
  # We use the standard error of the mean for the CIs
  res <- comparison %>%
    summarise(
      mean_cv_rum = mean(cv, na.rm = TRUE),
      std_dev_rum = sd(cv, na.rm = TRUE),
      n = n(),
      # Standard Error = SD / sqrt(n)
      se_rum = std_dev_rum / sqrt(n),
      # 95% CI calculation (Mean +/- 1.96 * SE) I do this to compare the the same CI in the MDCEV
      ci_lo_2.5_rum = mean_cv_rum - (1.96 * se_rum),
      ci_hi_97.5_rum = mean_cv_rum + (1.96 * se_rum)
    )
  
  return(res)
}

# Function to process data for a single year
process_year <- function(year_input) {
  data_year <- reservation_data %>% filter(year == year_input)
  
  unique_visits <- data_year %>%
    filter(!is.na(CheckinDate)) %>%
    distinct(id, parkname, CheckinDate) %>%
    group_by(id, parkname) %>%
    summarize(VisitCount = n(), .groups = "drop")
  
  all_combinations <- expand.grid(
    id = unique(data_year$id),
    parkname = unique(data_year$parkname),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  visit_data <- all_combinations %>%
    left_join(unique_visits, by = c("id", "parkname")) %>%
    mutate(VisitCount = ifelse(is.na(VisitCount), 0, VisitCount))
  
  person_info <- data_year %>%
    select(id, Permittee, zip, Zip, NoOfGuests, days_visited, year) %>%
    distinct()
  
  long_resdata_count <- left_join(visit_data, person_info, by = "id")
  
  long_resdata_count <- long_resdata_count %>%
    mutate(island_park = case_when(
      parkname == "Ainapo Cabin" ~ "Hawaii",
      parkname == "Hipalau" ~ "Kauai",
      parkname == "Kaluahaulu" ~ "Kauai",
      parkname == "Kamananui Trail" ~ "Oahu",
      parkname == "Kaunala Trail" ~ "Oahu",
      parkname == "Kawaikoi" ~ "Kauai",
      parkname == "Keanakolu Ranger & Bunkhouse Cabins" ~ "Hawaii",
      parkname == "Kuaokala Trail" ~ "Oahu",
      parkname == "Kulanaahane Trail" ~ "Oahu",
      parkname == "Kuliouou Trail" ~ "Oahu",
      parkname == "Lonomea" ~ "Kauai",
      parkname == "Maakua Ridge Trail" ~ "Oahu",
      parkname == "Manana Trail" ~ "Oahu",
      parkname == "Peacock Flats Campsite" ~ "Oahu",
      parkname == "Sugi Grove" ~ "Kauai",
      parkname == "Waialae Cabin Campsite" ~ "Kauai",
      parkname == "Waikoali" ~ "Kauai",
      parkname == "Waikolu" ~ "Molokai",
      parkname == "Waimano Trail" ~ "Oahu",
      parkname == "Waimanu Campsite" ~ "Hawaii",
      parkname == "Wiliwili" ~ "Kauai",
      parkname == "Wiliwilinui Ridge Trail" ~ "Oahu",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ))
  
  long_resdata_count <- long_resdata_count[order(long_resdata_count$id, long_resdata_count$parkname), ]
  long_resdata_count$parkname_numeric <- as.integer(factor(long_resdata_count$parkname, levels = unique(long_resdata_count$parkname)))
  
  long_resdata_count <- long_resdata_count %>%
    distinct(id, parkname, .keep_all = TRUE)
  
  final_data <- long_resdata_count %>%
    left_join(
      intra_island_distance %>%
        select(zip, parkname, income, residents_distance_meters, residents_duration, island_live),
      by = c("zip", "parkname")
    )
  
  final_data <- final_data %>%
    group_by(id) %>%
    mutate(visited_another_island = ifelse(any(island_park != island_live & VisitCount > 0), 1, 0)) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  final_data$travel_distance_km <- (final_data$residents_distance_meters / 1000) * 2
  
  
  return(final_data)
}

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(mlogit)
library(stringr)
library(readr)

years <- 2018:2023

final_comparison_all_years <- map_df(years, function(yr) {
  
  message(paste(">>> Processing Year:", yr))
  
  # 0. Get current year data
  current_data <- get(paste0("final_data_", yr))
  
  # 1. Create unique choice occasions for every visit
  data_rum_raw <- current_data %>%
    filter(VisitCount > 0) %>%
    uncount(VisitCount) %>%
    group_by(id) %>%
    mutate(choice_id = paste0(id, "_", row_number())) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    select(choice_id, id, chosen_park = parkname) %>%
    expand_grid(parkname = unique(current_data$parkname)) %>%
    mutate(choice = (chosen_park == parkname)) %>%
    left_join(current_data %>% select(id, parkname, price, travel_distance_km), 
              by = c("id", "parkname"))

  # 2. Add Stay-at-Home Option
  outside_home <- data_rum_raw %>%
    distinct(choice_id, id) %>%
    mutate(parkname = "Stay_Home", choice = FALSE, price = 0, travel_distance_km = 0)
  
  data_rum_combined <- bind_rows(data_rum_raw, outside_home)

  # 3. Model Estimation - Pushed to Global for the function to see
  df_idx_local <- dfidx(data_rum_combined, idx = list("choice_id", "parkname"), shape = "long")
  rum_mle <<- mlogit(choice ~ price | 1, data = df_idx_local)
  
  # 4. Prepare df_math - Pushed to Global for the function to see
  # This is the step that was likely missing or stale in your previous run
  df_math_local <- as.data.frame(df_idx_local)
  df_math_local$trip_id   <- as.character(idx(df_idx_local, 1))
  df_math_local$park_name <- as.character(idx(df_idx_local, 2))
  
  # Calculate V (Systematic Utility) using the CURRENT year's model
  df_math_local$V <- as.numeric(model.matrix(rum_mle) %*% coef(rum_mle))
  
  # Update Global df_math so calc_rum_welfare_manual uses the NEW year's V and trip_ids
  df_math <<- df_math_local

  # 5. RUM Welfare Calculations
  all_parks_list <- unique(df_math$park_name)
  all_parks_list <- all_parks_list[all_parks_list != "Stay_Home"]
  
  rum_results <- map_df(all_parks_list, function(p) {
    stats <- calc_rum_welfare_manual(p) 
    stats$parkname <- p
    return(stats)
  })

  # 6. Process Welfare Summary CSV
  welfare_summary_yr <- read_csv(paste0("welfare_summary_", yr, ".csv")) %>%
    mutate(parkname_numeric = as.integer(str_extract(policy, "\\d+"))) %>%
    left_join(
      current_data %>% select(parkname, parkname_numeric) %>% distinct(), 
      by = "parkname_numeric"
    ) %>%
    rename(mdcev_mean_per_person = mean)

  # 7. Final Join for the specific year
  year_comparison <- rum_results %>%
    mutate(rum_total_annual_loss = mean_cv_rum * n) %>%
    select(parkname, rum_mean_per_trip = mean_cv_rum, std_dev_rum, 
           ci_lo_2.5_rum, ci_hi_97.5_rum, rum_total_annual_loss) %>%
    left_join(welfare_summary_yr, by = "parkname") %>%
    mutate(
      year = yr,
      rum_annual_total = rum_mean_per_trip * sum(current_data$VisitCount, na.rm = TRUE),
      mdcev_annual_total = mdcev_mean_per_person * n_distinct(current_data$id),
      difference = rum_annual_total - mdcev_annual_total
    ) %>%
    select(year, parkname, rum_mean_per_trip, mdcev_mean_per_person, 
           rum_annual_total, mdcev_annual_total, difference, everything(), -policy)

  return(year_comparison)
})

title: rum
text:
This function calculate the rum welfare manual and is used for each year to get a comparison to the MDCEV.

calc_rum_welfare_manual <- function(target_park) {
  
  beta_price <- coef(rum_mle)["price"]
  
  # 1. Baseline Logsum
  ls_base <- df_math %>%
    group_by(trip_id) %>%
    summarise(ls_base = log(sum(exp(V))), .groups = "drop")
  
  # 2. Policy Logsum (Removing the park one by one and then use the consumer surplus using closed form based on the marginal utility of income
  ls_policy <- df_math %>%
    filter(park_name != target_park) %>%
    group_by(trip_id) %>%
    summarise(ls_policy = log(sum(exp(V))), .groups = "drop")
  
  # 3. Calculate CV for EVERY individual trip
  comparison <- left_join(ls_base, ls_policy, by = "trip_id") %>%
    mutate(cv = (ls_policy - ls_base) / -beta_price) # this is trying to compare to the MDCEV where we close and re-estimate
  
  # 4. Calculate Statistics across all trips
  # We use the standard error of the mean for the CIs
  res <- comparison %>%
    summarise(
      mean_cv_rum = mean(cv, na.rm = TRUE),
      std_dev_rum = sd(cv, na.rm = TRUE),
      n = n(),
      # Standard Error = SD / sqrt(n)
      se_rum = std_dev_rum / sqrt(n),
      # 95% CI calculation (Mean +/- 1.96 * SE) I do this to compare the the same CI in the MDCEV
      ci_lo_2.5_rum = mean_cv_rum - (1.96 * se_rum),
      ci_hi_97.5_rum = mean_cv_rum + (1.96 * se_rum)
    )
  
  return(res)
}

# Function to process data for a single year
process_year <- function(year_input) {
  data_year <- reservation_data %>% filter(year == year_input)
  
  unique_visits <- data_year %>%
    filter(!is.na(CheckinDate)) %>%
    distinct(id, parkname, CheckinDate) %>%
    group_by(id, parkname) %>%
    summarize(VisitCount = n(), .groups = "drop")
  
  all_combinations <- expand.grid(
    id = unique(data_year$id),
    parkname = unique(data_year$parkname),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  visit_data <- all_combinations %>%
    left_join(unique_visits, by = c("id", "parkname")) %>%
    mutate(VisitCount = ifelse(is.na(VisitCount), 0, VisitCount))
  
  person_info <- data_year %>%
    select(id, Permittee, zip, Zip, NoOfGuests, days_visited, year) %>%
    distinct()
  
  long_resdata_count <- left_join(visit_data, person_info, by = "id")
  
  long_resdata_count <- long_resdata_count %>%
    mutate(island_park = case_when(
      parkname == "Ainapo Cabin" ~ "Hawaii",
      parkname == "Hipalau" ~ "Kauai",
      parkname == "Kaluahaulu" ~ "Kauai",
      parkname == "Kamananui Trail" ~ "Oahu",
      parkname == "Kaunala Trail" ~ "Oahu",
      parkname == "Kawaikoi" ~ "Kauai",
      parkname == "Keanakolu Ranger & Bunkhouse Cabins" ~ "Hawaii",
      parkname == "Kuaokala Trail" ~ "Oahu",
      parkname == "Kulanaahane Trail" ~ "Oahu",
      parkname == "Kuliouou Trail" ~ "Oahu",
      parkname == "Lonomea" ~ "Kauai",
      parkname == "Maakua Ridge Trail" ~ "Oahu",
      parkname == "Manana Trail" ~ "Oahu",
      parkname == "Peacock Flats Campsite" ~ "Oahu",
      parkname == "Sugi Grove" ~ "Kauai",
      parkname == "Waialae Cabin Campsite" ~ "Kauai",
      parkname == "Waikoali" ~ "Kauai",
      parkname == "Waikolu" ~ "Molokai",
      parkname == "Waimano Trail" ~ "Oahu",
      parkname == "Waimanu Campsite" ~ "Hawaii",
      parkname == "Wiliwili" ~ "Kauai",
      parkname == "Wiliwilinui Ridge Trail" ~ "Oahu",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ))
  
  long_resdata_count <- long_resdata_count[order(long_resdata_count$id, long_resdata_count$parkname), ]
  long_resdata_count$parkname_numeric <- as.integer(factor(long_resdata_count$parkname, levels = unique(long_resdata_count$parkname)))
  
  long_resdata_count <- long_resdata_count %>%
    distinct(id, parkname, .keep_all = TRUE)
  
  final_data <- long_resdata_count %>%
    left_join(
      intra_island_distance %>%
        select(zip, parkname, income, residents_distance_meters, residents_duration, island_live),
      by = c("zip", "parkname")
    )
  
  final_data <- final_data %>%
    group_by(id) %>%
    mutate(visited_another_island = ifelse(any(island_park != island_live & VisitCount > 0), 1, 0)) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  final_data$travel_distance_km <- (final_data$residents_distance_meters / 1000) * 2
  
  
  return(final_data)
}

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(mlogit)
library(stringr)
library(readr)

years <- 2018:2023

final_comparison_all_years <- map_df(years, function(yr) {
  
  message(paste(">>> Processing Year:", yr))
  
  # 0. Get current year data
  current_data <- get(paste0("final_data_", yr))
  
  # 1. Create unique choice occasions for every visit
  data_rum_raw <- current_data %>%
    filter(VisitCount > 0) %>%
    uncount(VisitCount) %>%
    group_by(id) %>%
    mutate(choice_id = paste0(id, "_", row_number())) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    select(choice_id, id, chosen_park = parkname) %>%
    expand_grid(parkname = unique(current_data$parkname)) %>%
    mutate(choice = (chosen_park == parkname)) %>%
    left_join(current_data %>% select(id, parkname, price, travel_distance_km), 
              by = c("id", "parkname"))

  # 2. Add Stay-at-Home Option
  outside_home <- data_rum_raw %>%
    distinct(choice_id, id) %>%
    mutate(parkname = "Stay_Home", choice = FALSE, price = 0, travel_distance_km = 0)
  
  data_rum_combined <- bind_rows(data_rum_raw, outside_home)

  # 3. Model Estimation - Pushed to Global for the function to see
  df_idx_local <- dfidx(data_rum_combined, idx = list("choice_id", "parkname"), shape = "long")
  rum_mle <<- mlogit(choice ~ price | 1, data = df_idx_local)
  
  # 4. Prepare df_math - Pushed to Global for the function to see
  # This is the step that was likely missing or stale in your previous run
  df_math_local <- as.data.frame(df_idx_local)
  df_math_local$trip_id   <- as.character(idx(df_idx_local, 1))
  df_math_local$park_name <- as.character(idx(df_idx_local, 2))
  
  # Calculate V (Systematic Utility) using the CURRENT year's model
  df_math_local$V <- as.numeric(model.matrix(rum_mle) %*% coef(rum_mle))
  
  # Update Global df_math so calc_rum_welfare_manual uses the NEW year's V and trip_ids
  df_math <<- df_math_local

  # 5. RUM Welfare Calculations
  all_parks_list <- unique(df_math$park_name)
  all_parks_list <- all_parks_list[all_parks_list != "Stay_Home"]
  
  rum_results <- map_df(all_parks_list, function(p) {
    stats <- calc_rum_welfare_manual(p) 
    stats$parkname <- p
    return(stats)
  })

  # 6. Process Welfare Summary CSV
  welfare_summary_yr <- read_csv(paste0("welfare_summary_", yr, ".csv")) %>%
    mutate(parkname_numeric = as.integer(str_extract(policy, "\\d+"))) %>%
    left_join(
      current_data %>% select(parkname, parkname_numeric) %>% distinct(), 
      by = "parkname_numeric"
    ) %>%
    rename(mdcev_mean_per_person = mean)

  # 7. Final Join for the specific year
  year_comparison <- rum_results %>%
    mutate(rum_total_annual_loss = mean_cv_rum * n) %>%
    select(parkname, rum_mean_per_trip = mean_cv_rum, std_dev_rum, 
           ci_lo_2.5_rum, ci_hi_97.5_rum, rum_total_annual_loss) %>%
    left_join(welfare_summary_yr, by = "parkname") %>%
    mutate(
      year = yr,
      rum_annual_total = rum_mean_per_trip * sum(current_data$VisitCount, na.rm = TRUE),
      mdcev_annual_total = mdcev_mean_per_person * n_distinct(current_data$id),
      difference = rum_annual_total - mdcev_annual_total
    ) %>%
    select(year, parkname, rum_mean_per_trip, mdcev_mean_per_person, 
           rum_annual_total, mdcev_annual_total, difference, everything(), -policy)

  return(year_comparison)
})

V_all <- as.numeric(model.matrix(rum_mle) %*% coef(rum_mle))

# 2. Extract the long-format model frame and attach V
df_long_v <- as.data.frame(model.frame(rum_mle))
df_long_v$V <- V_all
df_long_v$choice_id <- idx(rum_mle, 1)
df_long_v$parkname  <- idx(rum_mle, 2)

# 3. Get metadata (id and zip) to ensure we don't lose them
# We grab this from your 'data_rum' object
metadata <- data_rum %>% distinct(choice_id, id, zip)

# 4. Define the list of sites (excluding Stay_Home)
sites_to_test <- unique(df_long_v$parkname)
sites_to_test <- sites_to_test[sites_to_test != "Stay_Home"]

# 5. Extract Price Coefficient
beta_p <- abs(coef(rum_mle)["price"])

library(purrr)

# Use map_dfr to loop through sites and bind results into one dataframe
welfare_2018_rum_ind <- map_dfr(sites_to_test, function(s) {
    
    df_long_v %>%
        group_by(choice_id) %>%
        summarise(
            # Welfare change = [ln(sum exp V_all) - ln(sum exp V_minus_s)] / beta_p
            ls_all = log(sum(exp(V))),
            ls_minus_s = log(sum(exp(V[parkname != s]))),
            .groups = "drop"
        ) %>%
        mutate(
            site = s,
            trip_welfare = (ls_all - ls_minus_s) / beta_p
        ) %>%
        # Join metadata to get 'id' and 'zip'
        left_join(metadata, by = "choice_id") %>%
        # Sum up welfare across all trips for each person
        group_by(id, zip, site) %>%
        summarise(mean_value = sum(trip_welfare), .groups = "drop")
})

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# 1. Get all unique users and all unique parks from current data
unique_users <- final_data_2018 %>% distinct(id, zip, income)
all_parks <- unique(final_data_2018$parkname)

# 2. Create the "52 Week" Skeleton
# This creates 52 rows for every single person in dataset
df_52_weeks <- unique_users %>%
  expand_grid(week = 1:52) %>%
  mutate(choice_id = paste0(id, "_w", week))

# 3. Identify the ACTUAL trips taken (from reservation data)
# need to assign these to specific weeks. 
# if no exact dates, we distribute them randomly or to the first few weeks.
actual_trips <- data_rum %>%
  filter(choice == TRUE & parkname != "Stay_Home") %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  mutate(week = row_number()) %>% # Assigns trip 1 to week 1, trip 2 to week 2
  ungroup() %>%
  select(id, week, chosen_park = parkname)

# 4. Join the actual trips into the 52-week skeleton
df_expanded_rum <- df_52_weeks %>%
  left_join(actual_trips, by = c("id", "week")) %>%
  # If 'chosen_park' is NA, it means they stayed home that week
  mutate(chosen_park = ifelse(is.na(chosen_park), "Stay_Home", chosen_park))
# 5. Expand to full choice sets (Parks + Stay_Home) for every week
df_rum_final <- df_expanded_rum %>%
  expand_grid(parkname = c(all_parks, "Stay_Home")) %>%
  mutate(choice = (chosen_park == parkname))

# 6. Re-attach Prices and Distance
# Note: Price for 'Stay_Home' is always 0.
df_rum_final <- df_rum_final %>%
  left_join(final_data_2018 %>% select(id, parkname, price, travel_distance_km), 
            by = c("id", "parkname")) %>%
  mutate(
    price = ifelse(parkname == "Stay_Home", 0, price),
    travel_distance_km = ifelse(parkname == "Stay_Home", 0, travel_distance_km)
  )

        mutate(week = row_number()) %>%
        filter(week <= 52) %>% # Safety check for skeleton bounds
        ungroup() %>%
        select(id, week, chosen_park = parkname)
    
    # 2. Build Choice Set
    df_rum_final <- df_52_weeks %>%
        left_join(actual_trips, by = c("id", "week")) %>%
        mutate(chosen_park = replace_na(chosen_park, "Stay_Home")) %>%
        expand_grid(parkname = c(all_parks, "Stay_Home")) %>%
        mutate(choice = (chosen_park == parkname)) %>%
        left_join(current_data %>% select(id, parkname, price, travel_distance_km), 
                  by = c("id", "parkname")) %>%
        mutate(across(c(price, travel_distance_km), ~replace_na(.x, 0)))
    
    # 3. Model Estimation - Update GLOBAL rum_mle_52
    df_idx_local <- dfidx(df_rum_final, idx = list("choice_id", "parkname"), shape = "long")
    rum_mle_52 <<- mlogit(choice ~ price | 1 , data = df_idx_local)
    
    # 4. Prepare df_math - Update GLOBAL df_math
    # This is the crucial step for your function to calculate correct logsums
    df_math_local <- as.data.frame(df_idx_local)
    df_math_local$trip_id   <- as.character(idx(df_idx_local, 1))
    df_math_local$park_name <- as.character(idx(df_idx_local, 2))
    
    # Recalculate V for the current year's model coefficients
    df_math_local$V <- as.numeric(model.matrix(rum_mle_52) %*% coef(rum_mle_52))
    
    # Push to Global
    df_math <<- df_math_local
    
    # 5. RUM Welfare Calculations
    parks_to_test <- all_parks[all_parks != "Stay_Home"]
    
    rum_results <- map_df(parks_to_test, function(p) {
        # This now finds the UPDATED rum_mle_52 and df_math
        stats <- calc_rum_welfare_manual(p) 
        stats$parkname <- p
        return(stats)
    })
    
    # 6. Process Welfare Summary CSV
    welfare_summary_yr <- read_csv(paste0("welfare_summary_", yr, ".csv")) %>%
        mutate(parkname_numeric = as.integer(str_extract(policy, "\\d+"))) %>%
        left_join(current_data %>% select(parkname, parkname_numeric) %>% distinct(), 
                  by = "parkname_numeric") %>%
        rename(mdcev_mean_per_person = mean)
    
    # 7. Final Join and Metrics
    year_comparison <- rum_results %>%
        mutate(rum_total_annual_loss = mean_cv_rum * n) %>%
        select(parkname, rum_mean_per_trip = mean_cv_rum, rum_total_annual_loss,
               std_dev_rum, ci_lo_2.5_rum, ci_hi_97.5_rum) %>%
        left_join(welfare_summary_yr, by = "parkname") %>%
        mutate(
            year = yr,
            mdcev_annual_total = mdcev_mean_per_person * n_distinct(current_data$id),
            rum_annual_total = rum_mean_per_trip * sum(current_data$VisitCount, na.rm = TRUE),
            difference = rum_annual_total - mdcev_annual_total
        ) %>%
        select(year, parkname, rum_mean_per_trip, mdcev_mean_per_person, 
               rum_annual_total, mdcev_annual_total, difference, everything(), -policy)
    
    return(year_comparison)
})
